title: Diabetic Foot Care: A Small Habit That Prevents Big Problems
summary: Diabetes can reduce sensation in the feet, which makes small injuries easy to miss until they become serious. A quick daily check prevents most problems.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Diabetic Foot Care: A Small Habit That Prevents Big Problems | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Diabetes can reduce sensation in the feet, which makes small injuries easy to miss until they become serious. A quick daily check prevents most problems.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Reduced sensation means injuries can go unnoticed for days.
Daily visual checks catch problems early, when they're easiest to treat.
Proper-fitting shoes and daily moisturizing reduce risk substantially.

paragraphs: Nerve damage from diabetes can reduce feeling in the feet, meaning a blister, cut, or pressure sore might not be felt the way it normally would — and can worsen significantly before being noticed. A daily visual check, even just a minute, is one of the highest-value habits in diabetes care.
Well-fitting shoes (checked regularly, since foot shape can change), daily moisturizing to prevent cracking, and prompt attention to any redness or sore are all simple, effective preventive steps.

nextSteps: Build a daily foot-check habit, even briefly.
Check shoe fit periodically rather than assuming it hasn't changed.
Contact the doctor promptly for any sore, redness, or wound that isn't healing quickly.
disclaimer: General guidance — a podiatrist can provide specialized foot care for those at higher risk.
